Tool use: **apply_patch**

<details>
        <summary>Apply patch for /workspaces/WaveX/firmware/esp32/components/ui/src/ui_existing_pages.cpp</summary>
      

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /workspaces/WaveX/firmware/esp32/components/ui/src/ui_existing_pages.cpp
@@
-        if (page->is_playing) {
-            keys[1] = {"Stop", []() {
-                // Stop audition
-                ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Stop audition requested");
-            }};
-        } else {
-            keys[1] = {"Audition", []() {
-                // Start audition
-                ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Audition requested");
-            }};
-        }
+        if (page->is_playing) {
+            keys[1] = {"Stop", [page]() {
+                // Stop audition
+                ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Stop audition requested");
+                wavex_sample_load_save_stop_audition(page);
+            }};
+        } else {
+            keys[1] = {"Audition", [page]() {
+                // Start audition using selected file index
+                ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Audition requested");
+                const wavex_file_entry_t* selected = wavex_file_browser_get_selected(page->file_browser);
+                if (selected && !selected->is_directory) {
+                    uint32_t selected_index = wavex_file_browser_get_selected_index(page->file_browser);
+                    wavex_sample_load_save_audition_sample_by_index(page, selected_index);
+                } else {
+                    ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No valid file selected for audition");
+                }
+            }};
+        }
*** End Patch
```

+// Refresh softkeys after state changes
+#include "../include/ui/ui_navigator.h"
@@
 bool wavex_sample_load_save_audition_sample_by_index(wavex_sample_load_save_page_t* page, uint32_t file_index)
@@
     wavex_sample_load_save_set_status(page, status_text);
+
+    // Refresh softkeys to reflect playing state (Audition -> Stop)
+    LV_LOCK();
+    wavex_ui::UINavigator::instance().softkeyBar()->setSoftkeys(wavex_ui::UINavigator::instance().active()->getSoftkeys());
+    LV_UNLOCK();
@@
 void wavex_ui_handle_sample_stop_response(bool success)
@@
-        // Update button text back to "Audition"
-        wavex_ui_update_hotkey_label(0, "Audition");
+        // Refresh softkeys to reflect stopped state (Stop -> Audition)
+        LV_LOCK();
+        wavex_ui::UINavigator::instance().softkeyBar()->setSoftkeys(wavex_ui::UINavigator::instance().active()->getSoftkeys());
+        LV_UNLOCK();
*** End Patch

- I refreshed the softkeys when audition starts and when a stop response arrives so the label toggles between Audition and Stop based on is_playing. Please rebuild and retest.

- In `firmware/esp32/components/ui/src/ui_existing_pages.cpp`, the Audition/Stop softkeys now call the real audition/stop functions using the selected file index.
- In `firmware/esp32/components/ui/pages/sample_load_save.cpp`, after a successful `wavex_sample_load_save_audition_sample_by_index`, the softkey bar is refreshed to show “Stop”. After `wavex_ui_handle_sample_stop_response(true)`, the softkey bar refreshes back to “Audition”.

Notes
- If the label still doesn’t flip: share logs around the audition request line and the SPI “Created packet” lines. Also confirm you see the softkey refresh (no errors from LVGL).
- Re: Daisy hang during playback: if it reproduces, please capture Daisy-side logs around WAV playback and any underrun/IO messages to trace.

Summary
- Pressing “Audition” now sends the play-index request and immediately refreshes the softkeys to “Stop”. When a stop response is received, the softkeys refresh back to “Audition”.

I’m going to modify Daisy’s `ProcessSamplePlayRequest` to send an ACK before doing any file I/O, to prevent SPI stalls during `OpenWav`. Then I’ll summarize and ask you to retest.

Tool use: **apply_patch**

<details>
        <summary>Apply patch for /workspaces/WaveX/firmware/daisy/src/comm/daisy_spi_filesystem.cpp</summary>
      

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /workspaces/WaveX/firmware/daisy/src/comm/daisy_spi_filesystem.cpp
@@
 void ProcessSamplePlayRequest(const char* file_path)
 {
     using namespace WaveX::Protocol;
     using namespace WaveX::AudioEngine;
@@
-    // Stop any current playback first
-    CloseWav();
+    // Send immediate ACK to keep SPI responsive while we start playback
+    {
+        AckMessage ack;
+        ack.serial_id = 0; // TODO: Get actual serial ID if needed
+        uint8_t response_buffer[MAX_PKT_SIZE];
+        size_t pkt_size = ProtocolHandler::CreateAckPacket(response_buffer, sizeof(response_buffer), ack);
+        Spi_SendPreCreatedPacket(response_buffer, pkt_size);
+    }
+
+    // Stop any current playback first (may touch filesystem/audio state)
+    CloseWav();
@@
-    // Start playback using the existing WAV playback system
+    // Start playback using the existing WAV playback system
     if (!OpenWav(file_path)) {
         if (s_hw) {
             s_hw->PrintLine("DAISY: Failed to open WAV file for playback: '%s'", file_path);
         }
         
         // Send error response
         ErrorMessage error;
         error.code = 1; // File open error
         strncpy(error.msg, "Failed to open WAV file", sizeof(error.msg) - 1);
         error.msg[sizeof(error.msg) - 1] = '\0';
         
         uint8_t response_buffer[MAX_PKT_SIZE];
         size_t pkt_size = ProtocolHandler::CreateErrorPacket(response_buffer, sizeof(response_buffer), error);
         Spi_SendPreCreatedPacket(response_buffer, pkt_size);
         return;
     }
-
-    // Send success acknowledgment
-    AckMessage ack;
-    ack.serial_id = 0; // TODO: Get actual serial ID
-    
-    uint8_t response_buffer[MAX_PKT_SIZE];
-    size_t pkt_size = ProtocolHandler::CreateAckPacket(response_buffer, sizeof(response_buffer), ack);
-    Spi_SendPreCreatedPacket(response_buffer, pkt_size);
     
     if (s_hw) {
         s_hw->PrintLine("DAISY: Sample playback started successfully for: '%s'", file_path);
     }
 }
*** End Patch
```

Model: GPT-5 Code Assistant, 32k context, Transformer (rev. 2025-09)

- I adjusted Daisy to send an ACK immediately upon receiving a play request, before doing any file I/O, to keep the SPI pipeline responsive and avoid a stall during `OpenWav`.

Please rebuild/flash and test again:
- Try Audition; Daisy should no longer hang at start. You should see an ACK quickly, followed by either playback or an error if the file failed to open.
- The softkey label should flip to “Stop” on audition start and back to “Audition” on stop response.

If it still hangs:
- Share Daisy logs around “ProcessSamplePlayIndexRequest”/“ProcessSamplePlayRequest” and any file I/O messages, plus ESP logs around “Created packet” and “SPI transaction completed successfully.”
- If the hang happens even before audio starts, we can also move the immediate ACK into `ProcessSamplePlayIndexRequest` before path lookup to rule out filesystem blocking on index-to-path resolution.
